title: Feminist Scholarship and NGE
wikitext:
[]: Feminist Scholarship and NGE             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 04, 2011 3:30 pm Okay, we've already seen how not to do this. But recent events have made me curious: has anyone seriously addressed this topic in an academic/scholarly context? Let me explain. When undertaking such an endeavor the primary question one must address is "why am I doing this?" At an undergrad level the answer will usually involve a class assignment, but after that the question demands a more thoughtful response. I can think of four such responses offhand: 1. To understand the goals of the work's creators and evaluate the extent to which they achieved those goals; 2. To understand how the work's creators might have been influenced unconsciously by various social forces, and to investigate the implications thereof (i.e. Death of Author properly applied); 3. To understand the context in which the work was created, as well as the issues it addresses and how it interacts with the body of work that came before it; 4. To understand the work's influence upon the culture that receives it. This last question can be asked even in the context of an audience for whom the work was not intended, as it addresses the audience that receives the work rather than the work itself. Ideas for specific sorts of questions can be found here and here ; I don't really want to delve too deeply into specifics here, though. I'm more interested in learning whether and to what extent NGE has been addressed in an academic context. I suspect most, if not all of the scholarship on the topic will be limited to Japan, since that is the audience most familiar with the show, its context, and the themes it addresses. But I'm not opposed to Western works as long as they make an effort to understand the work and the environment that created it. So, my questions: First, what scholarship on the topic actually exists? Second, is it any good, given the above criteria or other, more robust standards? Third, how much, if any, is available in English? I realize I can practice my Google-Fu here, but that won't put works in my hand or separate the wheat from the chaff. I'm hoping some of our more erudite posters have actually read some of this stuff and can point me toward the works that are worth the effort. It also occurs to me that academia might not be the best place to go to inquire about the subject; as such, competent blog posts (i.e. works that are both insightful and well cited) would also work for my purposes here. Ideally I would like to create an index of all such works and throw it up on a wiki page, assuming someone hasn't beaten me to it (in which case this whole topic is a waste of time and the thread can be safely junked). Maybe that will help us avoid more unpleasantness in the future. Also, getting educated on the topic wouldn't be a bad thing at all IMO. Long ramble is long, but hopefully you get the idea. Any takers?

[]: Re: Feminist Scholarship and NGE - ... Why? Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Nov 04, 2011 7:58 pm Bagheera wrote: Okay, we've already seen how not to do this.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 04, 2011 8:13 pm Yes, though thanks for pointing out the thread; some of our resident elitist snobs Chill, I use the term affectionately! dropped some useful names before the thing was locked. I'll have to check 'em out.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Nov 04, 2011 9:01 pm For an anime, Evangelion may have the most number of "serious studies" books ranging from mythology and religion, psychology, gender studies, to social studies and fandom research published in Japan. I remember reading at least a dozen of such books back in late 90s and it is just a small part of the whole output. They may not be up onto a rigorous academic study level, but serious discussions and research nonetheless (Some examples can be found in this blog article http://asitamoikite.blog95.fc2.com/blog-entry-158.html ). On the other hand, I am not quite sure how it is like in the west....

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 04, 2011 9:15 pm It looks interesting, buuuuuuut . . . moonspeak.

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Fri Nov 04, 2011 9:15 pm I don't think that evangelion has been dicussed as much as it has been I n Japan. Most adults I know just blow off all anime and don't take it seriously they either regard it as a children's cartoon or tentical porn. Where I live to watch anime is taboo it is very hard for people to take anime seriously mostly I just get laughed at when I try to get a serious intelectually dicussion and get told never to bring it up again. During my last week of high school I did a project on the kawrou ode to joy scene. I talked about the themes and other main points of the scene but The class just laughed called me and called me a nerd at least the teacher kind of liked the project he gave me a 75 and told me not to play cartoons in class ever again. Needless to say where I am from no one takes anime seriously

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Nov 06, 2011 5:31 pm I would love to see a Eva book that's written by someone who takes the history of the show, the people who worked on it, and what worked and didn't worked about the series seriously. And by seriously, I mean spent years getting in contact with everyone who worked on the show and find out what was going on at Ganiax during this time, the whole Aum thing behind the scenes, find out what the employees thought about the personalities of the more important members of the staff, etc. Basically, it would make my day if an anime version of Hollywood Cartoons: American Animation in its' Golden Age ever gets made.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Tue Nov 08, 2011 9:03 pm Yeah, that would be nice, but you're not going to get that. On the plus side, you could probably whip up a decent book or at least long essay with all the existing sources in English, 90% of which are completely omitted from existing English articles.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Wed Nov 09, 2011 6:57 am gwern wrote: Yeah, that would be nice, but you're not going to get that. On the plus side, you could probably whip up a decent book or at least long essay with all the existing sources in English, 90% of which are completely omitted from existing English articles.

[]:             Quote Post by peterw5 » Tue Nov 15, 2011 1:31 am I honestly didn't find much feminist scholarship on NGE when I was researching my paper. Some of the papers I read mentioned a few things about NGE and feminism, but it wasn't the main topic of the paper. If you are interested in what I found you can look at the "sources" page of my paper. I don't think it's what you're (Bagheera) looking for though. Most of it is American or British scholarship, and thus is from a Western perspective. If you're looking for stuff about the Japanese cultural context of NGE and the author's intent perhaps you could check that guy "V"'s website. I hear he's all about that. I think his website is called "Revolution of Evangelion."

[]: Not worth it. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Nov 15, 2011 3:20 am peterw5 wrote: If you're looking for stuff about the Japanese cultural context of NGE and the author's intent perhaps you could check that guy "V"'s website. I hear he's all about that.

[]:             Quote Post by peterw5 » Tue Nov 15, 2011 3:42 am Is "V" not liked around these parts, or something? Anyway, where would I find this information here, since you say it is here?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Nov 15, 2011 3:47 am peterw5 wrote: If you're looking for stuff about the Japanese cultural context of NGE and the author's intent perhaps you could check that guy "V"'s website. I hear he's all about that. I think his website is called "snausages."

[]: Covering the bases. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Nov 15, 2011 3:52 am peterw5 wrote: Is "V" not liked around these parts, or something?

[]:             Quote Post by peterw5 » Tue Nov 15, 2011 3:55 am Thanks Alaska Slim.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Nov 15, 2011 3:04 pm V is a blowhard with a nauseating superiority complex. His articles have been copied almost word-for-word from our own wiki and a few other sources.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Nov 15, 2011 3:33 pm Enough digression about this quondam forum member

[]: It's still used, go figure. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Nov 15, 2011 4:01 pm ^ Read that first as "Gundam", than as "Quantum", than something reminiscent of "Quota", and then I was like "ohhh..." But yes, I see I indulged too much on that, so I'll try to give the topic a boost: Saving Humanity through Gender Reversal : A Feminist Interpretation of Shinseiki Evangelion Also, Evangelical Feminism and the Bible, part I Surprisingly, the word "Evangelion" is used, and it's not in reference to this . SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: It's still used, go figure. Bl             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Nov 15, 2011 6:42 pm Alaska Slim wrote: Saving Humanity through Gender Reversal : A Feminist Interpretation of Shinseiki Evangelion
